title: Case When
description: Implement a multi-way branch using the case/when syntax based on a simple score value.
problemStatement:
The switch of Ruby! Assign `"A"` to a variable `grade`. Use a `case` statement to print `"Excellent"` if the grade is `"A"`, and `"Good"` for everything else (`else`).

**Requirement:** Use `case`, `when`, and `else`.
hints: `case` statements in Ruby are clean and don't need 'break' statements.
solutionCode:
grade = "A"
case grade
when "A"
  puts "Excellent"
else
  puts "Good"
end
